task_context.description: This is a comparative detector design and performance evaluation task for high-energy physics instrumentation. The user wants to design and simulate three different homogeneous electromagnetic calorimeters using different crystal materials (PbWO4, BGO, and CsI) to determine which performs best for precision electron and photon measurements at a future electron-positron collider. The task involves Monte Carlo simulation using Geant4 to characterize detector performance across multiple metrics including energy resolution, shower containment, and spatial resolution. The evaluation requires parameterizing energy resolution using the standard calorimeter resolution formula and measuring shower development characteristics to make an evidence-based recommendation for the FCC-ee electromagnetic calorimeter design.
task_context.goal: Determine the optimal crystal material choice for an electromagnetic calorimeter at the FCC-ee collider by comparing PbWO4, BGO, and CsI performance characteristics
constraints: Use homogeneous calorimeter design (no absorber material)
Use tractable simulation parameters for computational feasibility
Optimize for precision electron and photon measurements

## Executive Summary

This report presents the results of a comprehensive Monte Carlo simulation study comparing three crystal materials (PbWO₄, BGO, and CsI) for electromagnetic calorimeter design at the Future Circular Collider electron-positron (FCC-ee) experiment. observations.content: QUALITY GATE FAILED: deterministic physics violations (2). Extracted metrics: {'PbWO4_stochastic_term_a': '0.0099', 'PbWO4_constant_term_b': '0.0104', 'PbWO4_resolution_1GeV': '1.80%', 'PbWO4_resolution_10GeV': '1.33%', 'PbWO4_containment_1GeV': '99.1%', 'PbWO4_containment_10GeV': '99.0%', 'PbWO4_spatial_r90_1GeV': '24.0 mm', 'BGO_stochastic_term_a': '0.0094', 'BGO_constant_term_b': '0.0128', 'BGO_resolution_1GeV': '1.59%', 'BGO_resolution_10GeV': '1.32%', 'BGO_containment_1GeV': '98.9%', 'BGO_containment_10GeV': '98.6%', 'CsI_constant_term_b': '0.0217', 'CsI_resolution_1GeV': '2.25%', 'CsI_resolution_10GeV': '2.17%', 'CsI_containment_1GeV': '97.4%', 'CsI_containment_10GeV': '95.9%', 'total_events_simulated': '180,000', 'total_hits_recorded': '2.49 billion', 'recommended_material': 'PbWO4'}. Verifier reasoning: All three crystal materials (PbWO4, BGO, CsI) were successfully simulated and analyzed. Energy resolution fits were performed with proper parameterization: PbWO4 shows stochastic term a=0.0099 and constant term b=0.0104, BGO shows a=0.0094 and b=0.0128, CsI shows constant term b=0.0217. Energy containment is excellent for all materials (>98% for PbWO4/BGO, >95% for CsI). Hit statistics are robust with 60,000 events per material and millions of hits recorded. Spatial resolution analysis shows expected Molière radius scaling. Final recommendation clearly identifies PbWO4 as optimal choice based on superior energy resolution and containment. All requested deliverables exist: geometry files, simulation data, analysis results, and comparative recommendation.
